checks.a_analysis: The output content aligns with the headers and text found in the source PDF. No evidence of fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or entirely invented section headings was found. The metadata title is acceptable as an AI-generated descriptive field.
No fabricated phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs were found in the output. The URL present in the source was omitted rather than fabricated.

All three pages of the source PDF are represented in the output. While Page 3 is heavily truncated, the section heading 'Tasks' and high-level categories are present, which categorizes the defect under text omission (C5) rather than a missing section.
There are no tables in the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'Tasks' section on Page 3 is missing nearly all of its content. The source lists multiple bullet points and sub-points under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming', but the output only retains those two top-level words. The missing text includes 'Infrastructure buildout', replication tasks, 'EPA parcels project implementation', 'Elevation download from Geoportal imagery tab', and 'Website redesign/integration'. This constitutes an omission of approximately 80% of the section's content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Infrastructure buildout; Replicate data between web servers; Replicate all map/image services between data centers; Replicate geodatabases between DB servers; EPA parcels project implementation; Elevation download from Geoportal imagery tab; Website redesign/integration; NC OneMap; Geoportal

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: On Page 2, the output includes the text 'Geoportal Website:' but omits the actual URL string 'data.nconemap.com' that follows it in the source. This is a wording/content change that alters the information available to the reader.
checks.b_evidence_location: Data Releases section, Page 2, reading_order index 7

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source PDF appears only once in the JSON output, correctly reflecting the structure of the three slides without unnecessary repetition.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. H1 levels are used for the main titles of each slide ('NC OneMap Update', 'Data Releases', 'Tasks'), and H2 is used for a logical subsection within Slide 2 ('Natural Heritage Program'). No heading levels are skipped.
The source document does not contain any tables.

On Slide 2, the reading order is logically inconsistent with the source layout. The text 'Geoportal Website:', which appears as a footer element in the bottom left of the source slide, is assigned a reading_order of 7, placing it before the main content list starting at reading_order 9 ('Natural Heritage Program'). This disrupts the intended flow of information where the website link should follow the content items.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 2 bottom left footer: 'Geoportal Website: data.nconemap.com'
checks.b_evidence_output: The element with reading_order 7 'Geoportal Website:' appears before element 9 'Natural Heritage Program'.

checks.a_analysis: There are two significant list errors. 1) On Slide 3, the multi-level nested list is severely truncated. The source contains sub-bullets and sub-sub-bullets detailing specific tasks (e.g., 'Infrastructure buildout', 'Replicate data...', 'EPA parcels...'), but the JSON only captures the two top-level headers ('Ongoing', 'Upcoming') as a flat list. 2) On Slide 2, the parent-child relationship is broken; 'Natural Heritage Program' (a bullet in source) is converted to an H2, while its four sub-bullets are rendered as a separate flat list, losing their association as child elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 3 list items: 'Ongoing' > 'Infrastructure buildout' > 'Replicate data...'; Slide 2: 'Natural Heritage Program' is a parent bullet to 'Federal Lands', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: Slide 3 (reading_order 13) only contains 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming'. Slide 2 (reading_order 9-10) splits the heading from the list.

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'data.nconemap.com' present on Slide 2 is missing from the JSON output. While the introductory text 'Geoportal Website:' is present, the actual link data found in the source is omitted.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the 'Tasks' section from Slide 3 is significantly compromised. In the source PDF, this section is organized as a nested hierarchy with specific task items listed under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' categories. The HTML output collapses this structure into a simple two-item bulleted list, entirely omitting the sub-items. This significantly alters the visual organization and flow of the document, making it impossible for a user to find the same level of detail in the same logical place.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 3 displays 'Tasks' with sub-headings 'Ongoing' (containing 5 bulleted items) and 'Upcoming' (containing several nested bulleted items).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows 'Tasks' followed by a flat list of only two items: 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming'. All sub-bullets are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Tasks section, corresponding to PDF Page 3.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains no tables in either the source PDF or the HTML output.

The status icons (green checkmarks) used on Slide 3 to indicate completed tasks are missing from their original context in the HTML. Instead, a single, oversized checkmark image is rendered at the bottom of the section, disconnected from the text it describes. This constitutes a failure to correctly render meaningful images present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Three small green checkmark icons appearing next to task items under 'Ongoing' on PDF page 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: The checkmark icons are absent from the list; a single large green checkmark image appears at the bottom of the Tasks section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Tasks section, corresponding to PDF Page 3.

checks.a_analysis: No visual duplication of body content was observed. The presence of headings in both the table of contents and the main body is an expected accessibility feature.
